title: Ascochilus fasciculatus
taxonomy: Ascochilus fasciculatus belongs to the family Orchidaceae and subfamily Aeridinae. This species is classified under the genus Ascochilus, which contains several species of orchids found primarily in Southeast Asia. intro: Ascochilus fasciculatus, also known as the "Ascochilus fasciculatus" or "Ascochilus," is a species of orchid native to tropical Asia, particularly in India and Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ascochilus fasciculatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been reported to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The most common herbal preparations made with Ascochilus fasciculatus include decoctions, infusions, and tablets, which are used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and other health conditions.
